label: History
question: In what year was ASU founded as the Territorial Normal School?
fact: ASU was founded on March 12, 1885 — a full 27 years before Arizona became a state in 1912.

label: History
question: What was the original name of ASU when it first opened?
fact: It opened as the Territorial Normal School at Tempe, with just 33 students in a single four-room schoolhouse.

label: History
question: How many students attended ASU's first class in 1886?
fact: The first class had exactly 33 students, taught by first principal Hiram Bradford Farmer.

label: Campus
question: What is the oldest building on ASU's Tempe campus?
fact: Old Main was dedicated on February 4, 1898, and was also the first building in Tempe wired for electricity. It's now on the National Register of Historic Places.

label: Campus
question: Which famous architect designed the Grady Gammage Memorial Auditorium?
fact: Frank Lloyd Wright designed Grady Gammage — it was one of his last major commissions and was completed in 1964.

label: History
question: ASU's school colors have been maroon and gold since what year?
fact: Maroon and gold have been ASU's official colors since 1896.

label: Campus
question: ASU's famous Palm Walk is lined by how many Mexican Fan Palms?
fact: The 111 palms were planted under President Arthur John Matthews around the turn of the 20th century, creating one of ASU's most iconic walkways.

label: History
question: In what year did voters approve the name change to 'Arizona State University'?
fact: In 1958, Arizona voters passed Proposition 200 by a 2-to-1 margin, officially renaming it Arizona State University.

label: History
question: What was ASU called just before becoming a university?
fact: The school was called Arizona State College from 1945 until it became Arizona State University in 1958.

label: History
question: Who was ASU's first principal when classes began in 1886?
fact: Hiram Bradford Farmer was ASU's first principal and held the first class in the four-room Normal School Building.

label: Campus
question: The Lantern Walk tradition at ASU dates back to what year?
fact: The Lantern Walk, one of ASU's most cherished traditions, has been held since 1917. Participants walk through campus and up 'A' Mountain.

label: Campus
question: What is 'A' Mountain officially called?
fact: The hill is officially called Tempe Butte (also known as Hayden Butte). It has featured a large 'A' since 1938.

label: Campus
question: How many ancient Hohokam petroglyphs are found on 'A' Mountain?
fact: A Mountain contains approximately 500 petroglyphs left by the prehistoric Hohokam people.

label: History
question: Which U.S. President delivered the ASU commencement address in 2009 to a crowd of 70,000+?
fact: President Barack Obama delivered the 2009 commencement, calling ASU's commitment to diversity and access 'the hallmark of real success.' It may have been the largest U.S. graduation in history.

label: History
question: Which president spoke at the Tempe Normal School in 1911 and praised the institution?
fact: President Theodore Roosevelt visited on March 20, 1911, stating he had 'a special feeling for this institution' because seven men from his regiment came from it.

label: Campus
question: ASU's Manzanita Hall, built in the 1960s, was notable for being what at the time?
options: The first co-ed dorm in Arizona
The oldest continuously occupied dorm in the US
fact: The 15-story Manzanita Hall was, for a time, the tallest building in all of Arizona. It was also the first use of post-tensioned concrete construction in the state.

label: Campus
question: The Sun Devil Stadium sits between what unique geographical features?
fact: Sun Devil Stadium, built in 1958, is the only college football playing field located between two mountains — the Tempe Buttes.

label: History
question: The first graduate degree offered at ASU was in what field?
fact: The first graduate degree at ASU was offered through the Mary Lou Fulton Teachers College in 1937 — fitting for a school founded as a teachers college.

label: Campus
question: Which famous event was hosted at Grady Gammage Auditorium in 2004?
options: The final presidential debate between Kerry and Bush
answer: The final presidential debate between Kerry and Bush
fact: The third and final 2004 presidential debate between John Kerry and George W. Bush was held at Grady Gammage Auditorium.

label: History
question: When did the first ASU athletic rivalry game (later called the Territorial Cup) against UA take place?
fact: The Territorial Cup rivalry began on Thanksgiving Day 1899. The Territorial Normal School defeated UA 11-2 in their first meeting.

label: Sports
question: Which former ASU swimmer won 4 gold medals (and 1 bronze) at the 2024 Paris Olympics?
fact: Léon Marchand (France) had a historic Paris run — 4 golds and 1 bronze, setting 4 Olympic records. He became the third male swimmer in history to win four individual medals at a single Olympics.

label: Sports
question: How many total medals did ASU-connected athletes win at the 2024 Paris Olympics?
fact: ASU athletes brought home 9 Olympic medals in Paris 2024. Léon Marchand led with 5, plus 2 bronzes from Ilya Kharun and 2 from the Rogge sisters in water polo.

label: Sports
question: At the 2024 Paris Olympics, which country did Léon Marchand represent?
fact: Marchand swam for France, competing in the 400m IM, 200m butterfly, 200m breaststroke, 200m IM, and the 4x100m medley relay.

label: Sports
question: How many ASU-connected athletes competed at the 2024 Paris Olympics?
fact: 24 current and former Sun Devil athletes competed in Paris, representing 15 different countries across 7 different sports — the most of any Big 12 school.

label: Sports
question: Léon Marchand was a 10-time NCAA champion while at ASU. How many years did he swim for the Sun Devils?
fact: Marchand spent three seasons at ASU (2022–2024), winning 10 NCAA titles, 17 Pac-12 championships, and earning 21 CSCAA All-American honors before turning pro.

label: Sports
question: Which pair of ASU alumni sisters won bronze medals in water polo at the 2024 Paris Olympics?
fact: Bente and Lieke Rogge competed for the Netherlands women's water polo team. Bente scored a game-tying goal against Team USA in the bronze medal game.

label: Sports
question: Which famous golfer, an ASU alumnus, won 11 collegiate tournament wins at ASU and competed at the 2024 Paris Olympics for Spain?
fact: Jon Rahm won 11 collegiate tournaments at ASU — the second most in program history — and competed for Spain in Paris, tying for fifth in individual stroke play.

label: Sports
question: Phil Mickelson, another famous ASU golf alumnus, has won how many major championships?
fact: Phil Mickelson has won 6 major championships in his career, including 3 Masters titles, 1 PGA Championship, and 1 Open Championship.

label: Sports
question: Which legendary baseball slugger and all-time home run record holder attended ASU?
fact: Barry Bonds attended ASU before going on to a legendary MLB career. ASU is second among all NCAA universities with 117 alumni who've played in MLB.

label: Sports
question: Which NFL player and war hero who attended ASU left his professional career to enlist after 9/11?
fact: Pat Tillman played football at ASU from 1994 to 1997, then left the NFL to enlist in the U.S. Army after 9/11. He was awarded the Silver Star posthumously.

label: Sports
question: James Harden, the NBA star, played college basketball at which school?
fact: James Harden played one season at ASU before being selected 3rd overall in the 2009 NBA Draft by the Oklahoma City Thunder.

label: Sports
question: ASU's Sun Devils compete in which NCAA conference as of the 2024-25 season?
fact: ASU moved to the Big 12 Conference for the 2024-25 season after the Pac-12 dissolved.

label: Sports
question: Since 1952, how many total Olympic medals have ASU-connected athletes won across all Olympics?
fact: Since the 1952 Helsinki Games, Sun Devil athletes have won 66 Olympic medals total: 29 golds, 12 silvers, and 28 bronzes.

label: Sports
question: ASU's baseball program has sent how many players to MLB — second only to one other NCAA school?
fact: As of 2024, 117 ASU alumni have played in MLB, making it second among all NCAA universities. ASU also has the most inductees in the College Baseball Hall of Fame.

label: Sports
question: In what sport has ASU's women's program won a record 8 NCAA team titles — the most in its sport?
fact: ASU women's golf has won 8 NCAA titles, the most in the sport. ASU overall has won 25 NCAA team titles since 1965.

label: Sports
question: Which MLB Hall of Famer and Yankees legend played baseball at ASU?
fact: 'Mr. October' Reggie Jackson played at ASU before his legendary MLB career with the Oakland A's and New York Yankees, where he earned his Hall of Fame status.

label: Sports
question: Which ASU men's sports team won an NCAA championship as recently as 2024?
fact: ASU men's swimming won the NCAA title in 2024, the most recent of ASU's 25 NCAA team championships.

label: Famous Alumni
question: Which late-night TV host and comedian attended ASU?
fact: Jimmy Kimmel attended ASU. He's joined by fellow comedian alumni David Spade and Steve Allen, making ASU something of a comedy powerhouse.

label: Famous Alumni
question: Which fashion designer, known for her iconic handbag brand, graduated from ASU with a journalism degree in 1985?
fact: Kate Spade graduated from ASU in 1985. She met her future husband Andy Spade while at the university before going on to found her iconic fashion brand.

label: Famous Alumni
question: ASU has produced Nobel laureates and how many Pulitzer Prize winners among its faculty?
fact: ASU faculty includes 6 Pulitzer Prize winners, as well as Nobel laureates, 114 Fulbright Scholars, and 27 National Academy members.

label: Engineering
question: What is the official name of ASU's engineering school, named after a major donor?
options: Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering
answer: Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering
fact: In 2003, Ira A. Fulton — founder of Fulton Homes — donated $50 million to ASU's engineering college, which was renamed in his honor.

label: Engineering
question: How many undergraduate degree programs does the Fulton Schools of Engineering offer?
fact: The Fulton Schools offers 27 undergraduate degree programs and more than 50 graduate programs across 8 engineering schools.

label: Engineering
question: The Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering claims what national distinction?
options: Oldest engineering school in the US
Largest and most comprehensive engineering school in the US
First to offer online engineering degrees
answer: Largest and most comprehensive engineering school in the US
fact: The Fulton Schools is the largest and most comprehensive engineering school in the United States, with over 32,000 enrolled students.

label: Engineering
question: In what year was the first bachelor's degree program in engineering approved at ASU?
fact: The first bachelor's degree program in engineering was approved in 1956. The School of Engineering was formally created in 1958.

label: Engineering
question: How much was Ira A. Fulton's founding endowment to ASU's engineering school in 2003?
fact: Ira Fulton established the endowment with $50 million in 2003, transforming and expanding the college into one of the nation's leading engineering institutions.

label: Engineering
question: In 2023, ASU's Fulton Schools became the first university in the nation to offer degrees at all three levels in which engineering specialty?
fact: In 2023, the Fulton Schools became the first university in the US to offer a bachelor's, master's, and doctoral degree in manufacturing engineering.

label: Engineering
question: How many schools make up the Ira A. Fulton Schools of Engineering?
fact: The Fulton Schools comprises 8 engineering schools spread across ASU's Tempe, Polytechnic, and West Valley campuses.

label: Engineering
question: The Fulton Schools generated what percentage of all ASU patents in 2025?
fact: In 2025, the Fulton Schools generated 71% of all ASU patents, totaling 132 patents — reflecting its dominant role in ASU's research enterprise.

label: Engineering
question: What is the name of ASU Engineering's residential learning community specifically built for engineering students?
fact: Tooker House is a 7-story, 1,600-person co-ed living and learning community designed specifically for Fulton Schools students, featuring 3D printers, laser cutters, and on-site digital classrooms.

label: Engineering
question: What is ASU's world-class semiconductor research facility within the Fulton Schools called?
fact: MacroTechnology Works (MTW) is ASU's facility for research on semiconductors, advanced materials, processing and device fabrication at industrial scale.

label: Engineering
question: ASU's Fulton Schools has how much in annual research expenditures (FY2023)?
fact: The Fulton Schools has $265 million in annual research expenditures as of FY2023, up from $89 million in 2015, reflecting massive growth in research activity.

label: Campus
question: As of fall 2025, approximately how many total students are enrolled at ASU across all campuses and online?
fact: ASU had 160,051 students enrolled as of fall 2025, including 81,541 online students — making it one of the largest universities in the US.

label: History
question: ASU's mascot Sparky the Sun Devil was originally drawn by someone connected to which company?
fact: Sparky was first created by Bert Anthony, a former Disney employee. The concept of Sparky was introduced in 1951 and became the official mascot.

label: History
question: Before becoming the Sun Devils, which mascot did ASU use from 1922 to 1947?
fact: ASU's mascot was the Bulldogs from 1922 to 1947. Before that they were the Owls (1899-1922) and even earlier, the Normals.

label: History
question: ASU received 'Research 1' status — recognizing it as a top research university — in what year?
fact: ASU achieved R1 status in 1994, a remarkable feat for a university without a medical school or large agriculture programs at the time.

label: Campus
question: What is ASU's ranking distinction that it has earned from U.S. News & World Report year after year?
fact: U.S. News & World Report has consistently ranked ASU as the most innovative university in the United States.

label: Campus
question: ASU's Rare Books and Manuscripts Collection contains more than how many rare items?
fact: The ASU Library's Rare Books and Manuscripts Collection contains more than 34,000 rare items spanning over 40 centuries, including books small enough to fit in the palm of your hand.

label: Campus
question: How many campuses does ASU have in the Phoenix metro area?
fact: ASU has four campuses: the main Tempe campus, West (Phoenix), Polytechnic (Mesa), and Downtown Phoenix — plus regional learning centers across Arizona.

label: Sports
question: ASU's Ilya Kharun, who won 2 bronze medals at the 2024 Paris Olympics, competed for which country?
fact: Ilya Kharun, a current ASU swimmer at the time, competed for Canada. He won bronze in both the 100m and 200m butterfly events.

label: Sports
question: ASU has how many Pro Football Hall of Fame inductees among its alumni?
fact: Six ASU alumni are in the Pro Football Hall of Fame, including Curley Culp and Randall McDaniel. Football Hall inductees also include Danny White and others.
